Title: Enrichment of root-associated Streptomyces strains in response to drought is driven by diverse functional traits and does not predict beneficial effects on plant growth

Common_Genes.Applied_Species: Streptomyces isolates in sorghum root mono-association system
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Streptomyces spp. in association with Sorghum bicolor
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: osmolytes (e.g., proline; betaine; pipecolic acid; taurine)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports osmotic adjustment and drought-associated microbial fitness
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: sorghum roots and rhizosphere under drought
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher proP orthogroup copy number is positively associated with drought enrichment of Streptomyces in sorghum roots.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: pangenome-wide orthogroup copy-number association with drought enrichment phenotype using phylogenetic regression; mono-association sorghum root colonization assay under drought versus water; qPCR quantification
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Seven independent orthogroups annotated as proP showed positive association between copy number and drought enrichment. 2. proP encodes a proton symporter required for osmolyte transport and implicated in osmotic shift sensing. 3. The association suggests increased capacity to import drought-enriched root/rhizosphere osmolytes may improve bacterial fitness in droughted roots.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Association lacked strong phylogenetic signal for most orthogroups; gene was identified as a candidate mechanism rather than directly manipulated experimentally.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought versus irrigated conditions in gnotobiotic sorghum microboxes
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for selecting microbial inoculant strains with improved drought root persistence, but not sufficient to predict plant growth promotion.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Positive association with drought enrichment did not predict beneficial effects on plant biomass or water content.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing (JGI Genome Portal Proposal ID 502952); metabolomics (MassIVE MSV000093246); 16S rRNA data (NCBI BioProject PRJNA655744)

Common_Genes.Applied_Species: Streptomyces isolates in sorghum root mono-association system
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Streptomyces spp. in association with Sorghum bicolor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OmpR copy number was screened as a putative drought-enrichment-related trait, but no causal positive association with drought enrichment was established in the main results.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genome screening for copy-number variation of putative drought-enrichment-associated genes
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: response regulator transcription factor controlling outer membrane porins
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OmpR was included among candidate osmotic-stress-related genes surveyed across genomes. 2. Copy number varied among isolates. 3. The paper did not establish OmpR as a significant core determinant of nutrient-metabolite outcomes.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as a putative stress-associated gene during genome screening, but not supported as a top DE-associated orthogroup.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing (JGI Genome Portal Proposal ID 502952)

Common_Genes.Applied_Species: Streptomyces isolates in sorghum root mono-association system
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Streptomyces spp. in association with Sorghum bicolor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Kdp was surveyed as a putative osmotic-stress-response gene, but no significant direct causal relationship to drought enrichment was established in the main results.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Kdp was one of the osmotic-stress-related loci counted across isolate genomes. 2. Copy number varied among isolates. 3. It was not reported among the top orthogroups significantly associated with drought enrichment.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background candidate from genomic phenotype survey rather than a central experimentally validated core gene-metabolite determinant.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing (JGI Genome Portal Proposal ID 502952)

Common_Genes.Applied_Species: Streptomyces isolates in sorghum root mono-association system
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Streptomyces spp. in association with Sorghum bicolor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Trk was surveyed as a putative osmotic-stress-response transporter, but no significant direct causal relationship to drought enrichment was established in the main results.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Trk copy number was examined as part of genomic traits potentially linked to drought enrichment. 2. Variation was observed among isolates. 3. The paper did not identify Trk as a significant positively associated orthogroup in the final regression results.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background candidate from genomic screening; not a principal core determinant in results.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing (JGI Genome Portal Proposal ID 502952)

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Streptomyces isolates in sorghum root mono-association system
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Streptomyces spp. in association with Sorghum bicolor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: fatty acids / membrane lipids supporting drought fitness
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: membrane lipid biosynthesis contributing to cellular integrity during abiotic stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher fabG orthogroup copy number is positively associated with drought enrichment of Streptomyces in sorghum roots.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: pangenome-wide orthogroup copy-number association with drought enrichment phenotype using phylogenetic regression; mono-association sorghum assay; qPCR quantification of root colonization
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces β-ketoacyl-ACP intermediates to β-hydroxyacyl-ACP during the elongation cycle of fatty acid biosynthesis (FAS II).
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: essential enzyme; key control status not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Eight distinct orthogroups annotated as fabG showed strong positive association between copy number and drought enrichment. 2. fabG encodes an essential fatty acid biosynthesis enzyme in FAS II. 3. The data support membrane biosynthetic capacity as a candidate mechanism enhancing Streptomyces fitness in droughted roots.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene was implicated through comparative genomics and regression, not direct gene knockout/overexpression in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought versus irrigated conditions in gnotobiotic sorghum microboxes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful genomic marker to prioritize drought-persistent Streptomyces inoculants or engineer strains with improved abiotic stress resilience, though persistence does not ensure plant benefit.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced drought enrichment was not correlated with plant growth promotion.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing (JGI Genome Portal Proposal ID 502952); 16S rRNA data (NCBI BioProject PRJNA655744); metabolomics (MassIVE MSV000093246)
